name: Oedipus
description: Oedipus is a tragic hero in Greek mythology, best known for unwittingly fulfilling a prophecy that he would kill his father and marry his mother. Born to King Laius and Queen Jocasta of Thebes, he was abandoned as an infant due to the prophecy. He was rescued and raised by King Polybus and Queen Merope of Corinth, believing them to be his true parents. Fleeing Corinth to avoid the prophecy, Oedipus encountered and killed Laius at a crossroads, unaware of his true identity. He then arrived at Thebes, which was plagued by the Sphinx. Oedipus solved the Sphinx's riddle, earning him the throne and the hand of the widowed Queen Jocasta. He ruled Thebes successfully for many years, fathering four children with Jocasta. A plague struck Thebes, prompting Oedipus to investigate its cause. The investigation revealed the truth of his patricide and incest. Jocasta hanged herself upon learning the truth, and Oedipus, in despair, blinded himself. He was eventually exiled from Thebes and wandered Greece, accompanied by his daughter Antigone, until his death at Colonus. Oedipus's story is a powerful exploration of fate, free will, and the consequences of unchecked ambition and ignorance. His tale continues to resonate as a cautionary example of the human condition and the limits of human knowledge.
subtitle: The tragic king of Thebes
achievements: Uncovering the truth of his origins
Achieving a kind of heroic redemption in exile

keyMyths.title: The Prophecy
keyMyths.description: An oracle foretold that Oedipus would kill his father and marry his mother. This prophecy sets the stage for the entire tragedy, driving the actions of both Laius and Oedipus.

keyMyths.title: The Riddle of the Sphinx
keyMyths.description: The Sphinx terrorized Thebes, posing a riddle to all who approached: "What walks on four legs in the morning, two in the afternoon, and three in the evening?" Oedipus correctly answered 'man'.

keyMyths.title: The Discovery and Exile
keyMyths.description: When a plague strikes Thebes, Oedipus investigates and learns the terrible truth of his past. He blinds himself in anguish and is exiled from Thebes, bringing about the downfall of the Theban royal house.

sources.author: Sophocles
sources.description: The most famous and influential telling of the Oedipus myth.
sources.text: The most famous and influential telling of the Oedipus myth.
